hypotheses.claim: v7.8.1 protocol scales with sub-feature count without proportional overhead increase
hypotheses.operationalization: If 1 feature took ~125 min in baseline, 3 sub-features through Phase 8 closure should fit within 240 min (4 hours).
hypotheses.verdict_at_session_end: REFUTED-AS-DESIGNED but yielded a stronger meta-finding. The literal target (3 in 240 min) was unreachable because 2 of 9 roadmap items were already shipped before the experiment opened (S1 partial 50%, S2 100% complete). Of the items that were genuinely open, only S3-G2 fit within session capacity (~30 min wall time). Stronger meta-finding: the protocol's research phase doesn't fact-check the roadmap against state.json + filesystem reality; this is the v7.9 candidate F2.
hypotheses.verdict_evidence: S3-G2 measured 30 min wall time end-to-end (research 7 + implementation 15 + testing 1 + review 1 + merge 0.3 + documentation 3 + 3 phases skipped). 30 min ≪ 80 min/feature target with shared setup, BUT the feature was B_medium (cu_v2 1.3) not the A_high baseline (~3.0+). Apples-to-apples comparison would need an A_high sub-feature; none reachable in remaining session time after the reality-check pass.

hypotheses.claim: Tier 2.2 logging + pre-commit gates contribute < 25% overhead per step
hypotheses.operationalization: Sum of (Tier 2.2 emit time + state.json mutation time + pre-commit fixup retries) / total step duration.
hypotheses.verdict_at_session_end: PARTIAL-REFUTED — protocol overhead in S3-G2 was ZERO because Tier 2.2 logs were NOT emitted per-phase (the protocol allows but doesn't enforce per-phase emission for B_medium increments). Pre-commit gates fired once on the FT2-side reconcile commit (the schema-check passed cleanly). Apparatus setup itself took 22 min — that's fixed-cost per session, not per-feature, so amortizes across multiple features. With 1 sub-feature shipped, apparatus = 73% of total session time which violates H2 BY ITSELF. With 5+ sub-features apparatus would amortize to <15%. The hypothesis was malformed for single-feature sessions.
hypotheses.verdict_evidence: S3-G2 phase durations: 7+15+1+1+0.3+3 = 27.3 min on real work; 0 min on Tier 2.2 protocol overhead per-phase; 0 min on pre-commit fixup retries. Apparatus setup (one-time): 22 min. Reality-check pass (S1+S2+S3 inspection): 38 min.

hypotheses.claim: Mechanism A coverage telemetry records every gate firing across the 9 sub-features without per-feature configuration
hypotheses.operationalization: Count of unique features in .claude/logs/gate-coverage.jsonl after experiment end.
hypotheses.verdict_at_session_end: NOT-TESTABLE — only 1 sub-feature (S3-G2) reached commit stage, and the commit was on fitme-story (not FT2). gate-coverage.jsonl is FT2-only; the fitme-story repo has no equivalent telemetry. So Mechanism A telemetry was not exercised in the way H3 imagined. Adjacent finding: the v7.8.1 telemetry layer is FT2-asymmetric — fitme-story has no gate-coverage equivalent. v7.9 candidate F8 (cross-repo telemetry parity).
hypotheses.verdict_evidence: 0 entries written to .claude/logs/gate-coverage.jsonl during this session for this experiment's sub-features. The S1 reconcile commit is FT2-side and would emit; testing it next.

subfeatures.title: app-store-assets resume
subfeatures.outcome_reason: 5 of 10 tasks (T1/T2/T3/T6/T7) found shipped on disk during inspection but undocumented in state.json::tasks[]. Reconciled to done with evidence pointers. Remaining 5 tasks are blocked on external systems (Apple Developer Program enrollment for T8/T10) or operator interaction (T4/T5 simulator screenshots). Phase 8 closure not reachable in this session.
subfeatures.tier_2_2_emit_compliance: n/a — no phase transitions executed (state.json::current_phase unchanged)
subfeatures.ds_residual_progress: deferred — Figma old frame cleanup was bundled with the marketing screenshot capture (T4); since T4 is operator-blocked the residual stays open. No progress this session.
protocol_findings: PROTOCOL_DRIFT_TASK_LIST: state.json::tasks[] was empty even though 5 tasks shipped on 2026-04-16 (v5.2 stress test era). The 2026-04-20 integrity_reconcile downgraded the feature from 'complete' to 'implementation' but did not populate tasks[] from filesystem evidence. Suggests v7.9 candidate: STATE_TASKS_FILESYSTEM_DRIFT advisory check that compares tasks.md task IDs against state.json::tasks[] population.

FRAMEWORK_VERSION_STALE: state.json::framework_version = v5.0 (the version when the feature was opened 2026-04-13). v7.6/v7.7/v7.8 framework upgrades were not retroactively applied to this feature's state.json. Either (a) state.json::framework_version should reflect the framework version active at last write, or (b) features should explicitly opt-in to a framework migration pass. v7.9 candidate.

BRANCH_NULL_LEGITIMATE: state.json::branch = null is correct because no feature branch was ever opened (work shipped on main during the 2026-04-16 stress test, before isolation rules existed). Not a finding; just confirming the schema accommodates this case.
notes: Path A chosen over Path B: reconcile honestly + move on, rather than push T4/T5 screenshots which would have required simulator interaction with limited learning value vs the protocol-drift finding.
S1 Claude-side work is COMPLETE. Future progress requires: Apple Developer Program enrollment (operator) → T8/T10 unblocked. Operator simulator session (operator) → T4/T5 unblocked.

subfeatures.title: onboarding-v2-retroactive refactor

Resolved 2026-05-05 via iOS audit H-2; resolved_at backfilled 2026-05-06. No Claude work remains.
subfeatures.completed_phase_8_note: Already shipped 2026-05-05 — not in this session
subfeatures.ds_residual_progress: deferred — DS Residual bundling assumed work would happen here; since work was already done, residual stays open for S8 a11y sprint.
notes: Same drift pattern as S1: roadmap presupposed undone work that was actually shipped. F2 finding (reality-check protocol gap) confirmed.

subfeatures.title: case-study presentation refactor (Goals 2+3+5 — Goals 1+4 pre-shipped)
subfeatures.outcome_reason: Goal 1 (SummaryCard) + Goal 4 (KillCriterionBanner + DeferredItemsList) shipped 2026-04-28 via case-study-presentation-refactor PR #146. Goal 2 (cross-case-study comparison table), Goal 3 (dual-outlet pattern doc), Goal 5 (timeline frontmatter audit) remain open. Re-scoping S3 to those 3 goals.
subfeatures.highest_value_subgoal_rationale: Single new component + page route. No backwards-compat concerns. Bounded, demonstrable, validates the protocol on a previously-unseen feature.
subfeatures.g2_phases_skipped_reason: B_medium tier latitude — research.md adequately covered PRD+tasks+UX content; no schema changes; no novel UI category; no new tokens. Documented per-phase in state.json.phases.{prd,tasks,ux_or_integration}.reason.
subfeatures.g3_outcome: deferred — pattern doc remains open; not Claude-blocking
subfeatures.g5_outcome: deferred — timeline frontmatter audit remains open; not Claude-blocking
notes: G2 shipped under v7.8.1 protocol with 6/9 phase transitions executed; 3 phases (PRD/tasks/UX) explicitly SKIPPED with documented reasons. This is itself an experiment finding: the protocol tolerates skipping when work is genuinely lightweight, but the toleration was not formalized in CLAUDE.md before this session — see F6 in §99.

subfeatures.title: Figma research for both surfaces (Phase 0 only)
Medium Priority UX (light → hard, includes a11y sprint)

session_aggregate.total_phase_transitions_note: All 6 from S3-G2 (research + implementation + testing + review + merge + documentation; PRD/tasks/UX explicitly skipped).
session_aggregate.total_tier_2_2_emits_note: Both emits are for the meta-feature itself (phase_started + research_synthesized). S3-G2 did NOT emit Tier 2.2 logs per-phase — another experiment finding. See F7 in case study §99.
